question: Does the active reduced scalar optical-history cosmology admit any admissible observer-accessible continuation below x_crit?
supporting_internal_theorems: Paper 31 complete transfer map: observer-side scalar visibility/readout packaging sits on the reduced optical scalar-acoustic class, while Stage 2 local bulk evolution carries no observer-side readout leg.
Paper 31 Stage-2 characteristic-field inheritance theorem: no observer-side kappa/visibility/Cl/lensing operator acts inside the exact FULL Stage-2 update.
Paper 32 observer-readout transition theorem: the reduced visibility family is admissible iff x >= x_crit, closes uniquely to g=0 and exp(-kappa_c)=1 at x_crit, and is inadmissible below.
Paper 31/32 typed architecture: scalar lensing/Weyl remains a separate alpha=2 class and does not replace the scalar optical-history branch.
derived_facts: On the active reduced scalar optical-history branch, every observer-side last-scattering readout factors through the visibility family.
The visibility family is admissible exactly for x >= x_crit = Q^(-1/4).
At x = x_crit, the unique positive closure is zero visibility / transparent readout: g = 0 and exp(-kappa_c) = 1.
For x < x_crit, the present passive observer-side optical-history class is inadmissible because g < 0 and exp(-kappa_c) > 1.
Therefore there is no admissible observer-accessible continuation below x_crit within the active scalar optical-history class.
Any continuation below x_crit must leave that observer-side class and live, if at all, in hidden local bulk sectors not uniquely fixed by the current stack.

past_lightcone_check.conclusion: All currently observable redshifts satisfy x(z) >= 1.519 > x_crit.

claim_boundary: This is a theorem about the active reduced scalar optical-history observer map, not about arbitrary unreduced observables.
It does not derive a bulk equation of state below x_crit.
It does not exclude hidden local bulk continuation; it only proves that such continuation is not observer-accessible within the current scalar optical readout class.
final_verdict: The active reduced scalar optical-history cosmology terminates at x_crit in a unique zero-visibility closure. There is no admissible observer-accessible continuation below x_crit within that class. Any post-boundary continuation is hidden bulk physics outside the terminated observer-side readout class and is not uniquely derived by the present stack.
